methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $42M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Layup Parts is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Zack Eakin that has raised $2M+ to date. The comparable universe includes one public company (277810.KQ) and eight private peers spanning robotics and manufacturing, including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics. The narrow peer set limits traditional multiples-based valuation approaches.

Given data constraints, the Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable approach. Using total capital raised as a proxy baseline of $42M and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to next stage yields an implied valuation of $105M. A Peer-Median approach was not applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the robotics and manufacturing sector—while 12 peers exist in the broader sector, none met minimum density thresholds (need ≥3 datapoints) for credible median derivation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $89M to $121M, with a median point of $105M. This range reflects conservative ($89M) and upside ($121M) scenarios around the stage-progression output. Primary uncertainties include the reliability of the total-raised proxy as a value anchor in early-stage robotics, limited peer data for cross-validation, and execution risk typical of seed-stage companies. The single-method foundation suggests this valuation should be refreshed as additional comparable data or financial performance metrics become available.
